chat.author: Ricardo Signes
chat.text: Thanks volunteer notetaker, we appreciate you.

chat.author: Lisa Dusseault
chat.text: I mean thanks dan in this iETF not the last IETF

chat.author: Ricardo Signes
chat.text: one IETF's very like another when your head's down over your laptop

chat.author: Lisa Dusseault
chat.text: hm, IETF doesn't have a collaborative note-taker like hedgedoc, does it ? i've gotten used to ActivityPub/SocialWeb having a hedgedoc instance

chat.author: Lisa Dusseault
chat.text: ok so my vague memory was better than my internet search .  excellent!

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: there's an icon in meetecho that looks like a pencil in a box, that should (eventually) get you there too

chat.author: Lisa Dusseault
chat.text: 8 months between meetings and I forget everything

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: can't depth of presentation also be inferred by the rendering MUA based on what data is present?

chat.author: Arnt Gulbrandsen
chat.text: Neil: Replacement signal helps with text extraction for display in a summary or similar.

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: i understood that, i'm just saying that searching for X and (not Y) seems more awkward

chat.author: Hans-Jörg Happel
chat.text: Related to what ben says: an informational appendix/examples on known existing quoting practices might be helpful for implementers who plan to revisit their whole thread processing
